summary: LPC's own reporting describes landmark designations as "the outcome of an organic process with many steps," so the count completed in a given fiscal year is a snapshot shaped by how many multi-year cases happen to finish that year rather than a measure of program activity. The FY25 dip to four coincided with LPC advancing several cases -- including two proposed historic districts in Flatbush, Brooklyn and five individual landmarks in Midtown South -- that were designated only after the fiscal year closed.

sources.label: NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: decreasing, though this reflects case-completion timing, not less program activity
